documentation.content:
The public `/health` uptime check (`gridpulse-health`) is failing from more than one probe location — the `gridpulse` web service is down or shallow-degraded (Redis down, or scoring stale enough to flip the shallow `status` to `degraded`; #253).

Fires on `monitoring.googleapis.com/uptime_check/check_passed` for `check_id=gridpulse-health-162OIAwsIpE` when >1 probe location reports a failed check over a 10-min window (a single-location blip is ignored). This is the request-path counterpart of the deep-`/health` alert the README long listed as a follow-up.

**NOTE:** the `check_id` in the filter is deployment-specific — if the uptime check is deleted and recreated, GCP assigns a new id and this filter must be updated (`gcloud monitoring uptime list`).

**Runbook:**
1. `curl -s https://gridpulse.kristenmartino.ai/health` — the public liveness `{"status": ...}`; `degraded` points at Redis or stale scoring, no response at the service/deploy.
2. From an allowlisted IP, `curl -s '.../health?deep=1'` for the detailed body (Redis / last_scored / forecast_sample).
3. `gcloud run services logs read gridpulse --region=us-east1 --limit=200`.
4. Bad deploy → roll back the revision: `gcloud run services update-traffic gridpulse --to-revisions=<prev>=100`.

conditions.displayName: uptime check failing from >1 location (10 min)
